response: I am TinyGPT, a small language model trained from scratch on plain text documents.
A transformer is a neural network architecture that uses self-attention to model relationships between all tokens in a sequence simultaneously.
Self-attention lets each token look at every other token in the sequence and decide how much to focus on each one when building its representation.
Gradient descent is an optimisation algorithm that minimises a loss function by repeatedly moving parameters in the direction of steepest decrease.
Overfitting happens when a model memorises training data instead of learning general patterns, causing poor performance on unseen examples.
Tokenisation converts raw text into a sequence of integer IDs by mapping subword units to vocabulary entries.
A language model assigns probabilities to sequences of tokens and can generate text by repeatedly sampling the next most likely token.
Weight tying shares the parameters between the token embedding matrix and the output projection, reducing model size and improving generalisation.
Dropout randomly zeroes out activations during training to prevent co-adaptation, which helps reduce overfitting on small datasets.
Layer normalisation normalises the mean and variance of activations within each layer, stabilising training and improving convergence speed.
BPE stands for Byte-Pair Encoding, a subword tokenisation method that iteratively merges the most frequent adjacent token pairs.
Positional encoding adds information about the position of each token in the sequence, since transformers have no built-in notion of order.

prompt: Explain the MLP in a transformer.
response: The MLP in a transformer block is a two-layer feed-forward network with a GELU activation applied position-wise after the attention layer.
Mixed precision training uses 16-bit floating point for forward and backward passes and 32-bit for parameter updates, reducing memory use and speeding up computation.
Gradient clipping rescales gradients whose norm exceeds a threshold, preventing dangerously large updates that can destabilise training.
